steps.title: Summarize both measures
steps.content: The student's goal is to present an overview of the study's findings, not one isolated detail. The notes distinguish syllable rate from information rate: Spanish and Vietnamese had different syllable rates, but information rate was approximately consistent across all 17 languages.

steps.title: Use the study overview
steps.content: Choice A only says the researchers measured information rate, so it does not report the findings. Choice B confuses syllable rate with information rate by treating syllables per second as how quickly information was conveyed. Choice C invents a Spanish-Vietnamese information-rate comparison. The overview should state the central contrast: syllable rates differed, while all 17 languages conveyed information at roughly the same rate. The answer is D.
